channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This 10-second tweak will bring new life to any browser
item.description: When it comes to most home screens—on your phone, your computer, you name it—you’ve got endless-seeming options for customizing and taking control. The one canvas you can’t easily paint, curiously enough, is your browser’s New Tab page.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it’s the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Morga, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Donald Trump is losing on TikTok
item.description: TikTok’s influence has become undeniable in the 2024 presidential election, with both camps leaning full force into the idea that if you can’t beat ’em, join ’em. But that plan hasn’t worked out so well for Republican nominee Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Google, Meta, and TikTok shut down a Russian drone factory’s accounts over bombshell investigation
item.description: Google, Meta and TikTok have removed social media accounts belonging to an industrial plant in Russia’s Tatarstan region aimed at recruiting young foreign women to make drones for Moscow’s war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification: Pentagon Refines Program Rule, Industry Prepares for Implementation Challenges
item.description: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) final rule published by the Department of Defense; industry groups raise concerns about implementation challenges
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: How AI can help federal agencies make the most of limited budgets
item.description: In today’s budget environment, agency leaders are often asked to do more with less. The post How AI can help federal agencies make the most of limited budgets first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok approved election misinformation ads despite its own ban, says watchdog
item.description: Just weeks before the U.S. presidential election, TikTok approved advertisements that contained election disinformation even though it has a ban on political ads, according to a report published Thursday by the nonprofit Global Witness.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Helping the public aboard the AI bandwagon
item.description: When the first automobiles started making their way across our muddy, rutted roads, the public’s reaction was decidedly mixed. One car inventor remembers being called “crazy” for thinking his “fool contraption…will ever displace the horse.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ultimate guide to the best AI tools to make your life and work easier
item.description: Generative AI tools have been popping up everywhere and becoming second nature to use. That’s thanks in part, to a familiar user experience.
item.dc:creator: Sadiya Kherani, Fast Company

item.title: Meta Partners with Blumhouse Productions to Test Movie Gen AI Video Model
item.description: Meta teams up with Blumhouse Productions to test its generative AI video model, Movie Gen, in the film industry
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: An Alabama man has been arrested for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How you can help protect your aging parents from scammers
item.description: "They were raised to be a very trusting generation, a very rule following generation, so they make the assumption that everyone has their best interest at heart," Paula Daigle said.
item.dc:creator: Hayley Wielgus, NewsNation

item.title: Gluware Revolutionizes Network Automation with AI-Powered Features - A Game-Changer for NetOps
item.description: Gluware's latest update introduces AI-powered features, GitHub integration, and NetBox integration to revolutionize network automation and reduce downtime.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Global cybersecurity talent gap widens
item.description: Despite some recent positive news for tech hiring, a growing cybersecurity talent shortage threatens to hamstring businesses. Data from CyberSeek shows that in the U.S., some 265,000 more cybersecurity workers are needed to solve current staffing needs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: iOS 18: Maximize your privacy by turning off these iPhone settings now
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals some of the iPhone settings you may want to turn off to improve privacy after updating to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Big Tech turns to nuclear energy to power AI boom
item.description: Major technology companies are increasingly turning to nuclear energy as they attempt to keep up with the vast energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI) without derailing their highly publicized environmental goals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Cloud-enabled government: Seamless & secure data access
item.description: How is cloud adoption transforming federal agencies and driving mission success? The post Cloud-enabled government: Seamless & secure data access first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump thinks Google split would weaken US against China
item.description: The potential split up of Google that’s been proposed by the US Department of Justice (DOJ) could weaken the company, and thus the position of the US in its tech war with China, said former President Donald Trump, who suggested he may not break up the company if he wins the presidency again in Novem
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI isn’t really that smart yet, Apple researchers warn
item.description: While we wait for the Age Of Apple Intelligence, it may be worth considering a recent Apple research study that exposes critical weaknesses in existing artificial intelligence models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: Ride-hailing service Lyft announced it will offer discounted rides, bikeshare and scooters on Election Day, an initiative designed to increase transportation access for voters, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: AT&T and Broadcom near settlement in VMware support dispute
item.description: AT&T and Broadcom are close to resolving their legal dispute over VMware support services, according to a joint letter filed in the New York Supreme Court on October 11. The letter requests a postponement of the upcoming hearing, as both sides make progress toward a settlement.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk unveils Tesla's Robovan, Robotaxi, humanoid robots
item.description: Tesla's "We, Robot" event reveals the Robovan, Robotaxi and the Optimus humanoid robot. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson examines the company's latest innovations in autonomous technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Cato Networks extends SASE platform with digital experience monitoring
item.description: Cato Networks today introduced digital experience monitoring (DEM) capabilities that will augment the company’s cloud SASE platform with application traffic and network performance data that IT operations teams need to optimize end-user experiences.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
